最近，我们几个人走进手稿商店去看一些中世纪的家谱卷轴。我们在检查13世纪后半叶的皇家MS 14B V卷轴时，发现了一幅骑士与蜗牛搏斗的油画。这卷卷轴上有相当多的页边文字。我们的一位后中世纪同事注意到了一幅画，画的是一位骑士与蜗牛作战。
骑士大战蜗牛(摘自13世纪4/4世纪英格兰国王的家谱，皇家MS 14B V，第3膜)。
这让他感到奇怪，这让小组中的中世纪学者感到奇怪；肯定每个人以前都见过这种事情，对吗？正如任何熟悉13世纪和14世纪有照明的手稿的人都可以证明的那样，武装骑士与蜗牛搏斗的画面很常见，特别是在边缘部分。但这些描绘的无处不在并不会让它们变得不那么奇怪，我们对这些照片可能意味着什么进行了长时间的讨论。
骑士大战蜗牛II：边缘之战(摘自英国(萨福克)戈勒斯顿诗篇，1310年至1324年，增加MS 49622,193v成立。在我们之前的帖子中，我阅读了更多关于美丽的戈勒斯顿边缘的文章。)。
关于这些描述蜗牛战斗的意义，学术界有很多争论。早在1850年，声名显赫的藏书家巴斯塔德伯爵(Comte De Bastard)就提出了一种理论，即一只蜗牛的特殊边缘形象意在代表复活，因为他在两份接近拉撒路(Lazarus)饲养的微型手稿中发现了这一点。莉莲·兰德尔在她对这一主题的著名调查中提出，蜗牛是伦巴第人的象征，伦巴第人在中世纪早期因叛国行为、高利贷之罪以及“一般的非侠义行为”而受到诋毁。这种解读解释了为什么蜗牛如此频繁地与身穿盔甲的骑士对抗，但没有解释为什么蜗牛经常被描绘成这场战斗的输家，或者为什么这一特定的形象在非骑士的边缘变得如此受欢迎。这一解释解释了为什么蜗牛经常被描绘成这场战斗的输家，或者为什么这个特定的形象在非骑士的边缘变得如此流行--这是为什么蜗牛经常被描绘成这场战斗的输家的原因，或者为什么这个特定的形象在非骑士的边缘变得如此受欢迎。
骑士大战蜗牛III：极限格斗(来自布鲁内托·拉蒂尼的Li Livres Dou Tresor，法国(皮卡迪)，公元1315-1975，叶茨·汤普森MS 19，65R成立)。
其他学者则不同程度地描述了“骑士大战蜗牛”的主题，它们代表了穷人与压迫贵族的斗争，直截了当地表达了蜗牛作为花园害虫的讨厌名声，对社会攀登者的评论，甚至是女性性欲的性感象征。这些图像可能在某个时候意味着所有这些事情，甚至更多；重要的是要记住，正如迈克尔·卡米尔(Michael Camille)曾经写道的那样，他花了很多篇幅来研究这个主题：“边缘意象缺乏宗教叙事或偶像那样的图像稳定性”。他说，这个主题是丰富的视觉传统的一部分，我们今天只能不完全理解它--这并不是说这会阻止我们去尝试！
骑士大战蜗牛四：蜗牛攻击(出自英国玛丽女王诗篇，1310-1320年，皇家MS 2 B VII，148r版)
下面是更多我们最喜欢的大英图书馆图片，请告诉我们你的想法。你可以在下面发表评论，或者随时可以在Twitter上联系到我们@BL中世纪。
《蜗牛骑士大战蜗牛V：蜗牛的复仇》(出自法国南部史密斯菲尔德剧团(可能是图卢兹)，在英国(伦敦)增加了边缘场景，公元1300-c。1340，皇家MS 10 E IV，107R版)。
骑士大战蜗牛六：腹足类征服者(摘自英国(萨福克)戈勒斯顿诗篇，1310年至1324年，增加MS 49622,162v)。
骑士大战蜗牛七世：一场相当全面的失败(摘自一本零碎的小时书，英格兰(伦敦)，约1320-c。1330号，哈雷号6563号。62V-63R)。
骑士大战蜗牛八：这一次是猴子！(出自英国(萨福克)戈勒斯顿诗篇，1310年-1324年，加MS 49622，第210v页)。
骑士大战蜗牛九：只是为了好玩：一只兔子，猴子和蜗牛决斗(摘自荷兰哈雷弗罗萨特(布鲁日)，约1470-1472，哈雷MS 4379，23v)。
莉莲·兰德尔，“哥特式边缘战争中的蜗牛”，第37期，第6期(1962年6月)，第358-367页。
note_en:
Recently a group of us went into our manuscripts store to have a look at some medieval genealogical rolls.  We were examining  Royal MS 14 B V, an English roll from the last part of the 13th century that contains quite a lot of marginalia, when one of our post-medieval colleagues noticed a painting of a knight engaging in combat with a snail.
  Knight v Snail  (from a genealogical roll of the kings of England, England, 4th quarter of the 13th century,  Royal MS 14 B V, membrane 3)
  This struck him as odd, which struck the  medievalists in the group as odd; surely everyone has seen this sort of thing before, right?  As anyone who is familiar with 13th and 14th century illuminated manuscripts can attest, images of armed knights fighting snails are common, especially in marginalia.  But the ubiquity of these depictions doesn’t make them any less strange, and we had a long discussion about what such pictures might mean.
  Knight v Snail II:  Battle in the Margins (from the Gorleston Psalter, England (Suffolk), 1310-1324,  Add MS 49622, f. 193v.   Read more on the gorgeous Gorleston marginalia, in our previous posts.)
  There has been much scholarly debate about the significance of these depictions of snail combat.  As early as 1850, the magnificently-named bibliophile the Comte de Bastard theorised that a particular marginal image of a snail was intended to represent the Resurrection, since he discovered it in two manuscripts close to miniatures of the Raising of Lazarus.  In her famous survey of the subject, Lilian Randall proposed that the snail was a symbol of the Lombards, a group vilified in the early middle ages for treasonous behaviour, the sin of usury, and ‘non-chivalrous comportment in general.’  This interpretation accounts for why the snail is so frequently seen antagonising a knight in armour, but does not explain why the knight is often depicted on the losing end of this battle, or why this particular image became so popular in the margins of non-historical texts such as Psalters or Books of Hours.
  Knight v Snail III: Extreme Jousting (from Brunetto Latini's  Li Livres dou Tresor, France (Picardy), c. 1315-1325,  Yates Thompson MS 19, f. 65r)
  Other scholars have variously described the ‘knight v snail’ motif as a representation of the struggles of the poor against an oppressive aristocracy, a straightforward statement of the snail’s troublesome reputation as a garden pest, a commentary on social climbers, or even as a saucy symbol of female sexuality.  It is possible that these images could have meant all these things and more at one time or another; it is important to remember, as Michael Camille, who devoted a number of pages to this subject, once wrote: ‘marginal imagery lacks the iconographic stability of a religious narrative or icon’.   This motif was part of a rich visual tradition that we can understand only imperfectly today – not that this will stop us from trying!
  Knight v Snail IV:  The Snails Attack (from the Queen Mary Psalter, England, 1310-1320,  Royal MS 2 B VII, f. 148r)
  Some more of our favourite British Library images are below, and please let us know what you think. You can leave a comment below, or we can always be reached on Twitter at  @BLMedieval.
  Knight v Snail V:  Revenge of the Snail (from the Smithfield Decretals, southern France (probably Toulouse), with marginal scenes added in England (London), c. 1300-c. 1340,  Royal MS 10 E IV, f. 107r)
  Knight v Snail VI:  The Gastropod Conqueror (from the Gorleston Psalter, England (Suffolk), 1310-1324,  Add MS 49622, f. 162v)
  Knight v Snail VII: A Pretty Comprehensive Defeat (from a fragmentary Book of Hours, England (London), c. 1320-c. 1330,  Harley MS 6563, ff. 62v-63r)
  Knight v Snail VIII:  Switcheroo!  It's a Monkey This Time (from the Gorleston Psalter, England (Suffolk), 1310-1324,  Add MS 49622, f. 210v)
  Knight v Snail IX:  Just for Fun:  A Rabbit, Monkeys, and a Snail Jousting (from the Harley Froissart, Netherlands (Bruges), c. 1470-1472,  Harley MS 4379, f. 23v)
      Lilian Randall, ‘The Snail in Gothic Marginal Warfare’  Speculum 37, no. 6 (June 1962), pp. 358-367.

建造一座创新的新核电站的计划-从而重振陷入困境的美国核工业-和最近几周一样受到了打击：已经签约帮助建设核电站的36家公用事业公司中，有8家已经退出了协议。就在几个月前，犹他州联合市政电力系统(UAMPS)宣布，该项目的完工将推迟3年，至2030年。UAMPS打算从NuScale Power购买包含12个小型模块化反应堆的核电站。该公司还估计，成本将从42亿美元攀升至61亿美元。
UAMPS在犹他州、加利福尼亚州、爱达荷州、内华达州、新墨西哥州和怀俄明州拥有近40名会员。UAMPS的发言人拉瓦尔·韦伯(LaVarr Webb)表示：“该项目仍在进行中。”韦伯说，虽然UAMPS的一些成员已经退出，但“正在与一些公用事业公司进行有希望的讨论，以加入该项目或签订购电协议。”
然而，该项目的批评者表示，这些进展突显出，这座由NuScale Power设计、将在能源部爱达荷州国家实验室(DoE‘s Idaho National Laboratory)建造的核电站将耗资巨大。在温哥华不列颠哥伦比亚省大学从事公共政策研究的物理学家M.V.拉马纳(M.V.Ramana)表示，这么多公用事业公司选择退出该项目，他并不感到惊讶。他说，问题是为什么这么多人坚持这样做。他说：“他们应该看到不祥之兆，然后成群结队地离开。”
NuScale于2007年从俄勒冈州立大学(Oregon State University)剥离出来，并受益于能源部的支持。该公司设计了一种小型模块化核反应堆，该公司表示，与传统的千兆瓦功率反应堆相比，它将更安全、更便宜、更灵活。NuScale的每个小反应堆只能产生60兆瓦的电力。一座工厂将包含12个模块化反应堆，这些反应堆将在工厂建造并运往工厂现场。NuScale的工程师说，通过依靠自然对流来冷却反应堆，并省去了许多泵和管道，他们设计出了一种比传统动力反应堆更安全的反应堆。
此外，操作员还可以通过打开和关闭单个反应堆来提高和降低核电站的发电量。韦布斯说，这种能力对UAMPS很有吸引力，因为它试图减少对燃煤发电厂的依赖，减少吸热二氧化碳气体的排放。他说，UAMPS越来越多地转向太阳能和风能，但由于太阳并不总是照耀，风也不总是吹，公用事业公司仍然需要按需供应无碳能源。韦伯说：“我们的想法是，核能将补充、增强和支持你的可再生能源。”
然而，拉马纳认为，如果NuScale电厂不能持续满负荷运转，就每兆瓦时的能源成本而言，它的效率将会降低，运营成本甚至会更高。美国核管理委员会(NRC)前成员、缅因州和纽约州公用事业委员会前主席彼得·布拉德福德(Peter Bradford)表示，可再生能源加上电池的短期储存很可能是平衡供应的一种更便宜的手段。
韦伯反驳说，加州的能源市场已经表明，在可再生能源供应下降时，如果没有一些灵活的发电能力，公用事业公司仍然必须依赖碳密集型煤炭。韦伯说，这笔交易保护了UAMPS的客户，因为它明确规定了发电厂的最高电价为每兆瓦时55美元，这应该会使其与未来的天然气电价相竞争。他说，能源部将帮助确保这一比率，因为它最近敲定了一项计划，将承担14亿美元的工厂成本。他说：“如果(每兆瓦时)超过55美元，我们就不会建厂。”
布拉德福德质疑这种保证能有多可靠。他指出，上世纪80年代，华盛顿公共电力供应系统(Washington Public Power Supply System)同意在华盛顿建造几座核反应堆，这些反应堆远远超出预算，而且从未完工，导致了美国历史上最大的市政债券违约。布拉德福德说，公用事业特别容易受到这样的风险的影响，因为除了差饷纳税人，他们几乎没有收入来源可以用来弥补成本超支。他说：“这里不仅没有雄厚的财力，也没有财力雄厚的人。”
10月28日，犹他州的Heber Light&Amp；Power退出了该项目，就在犹他州邦蒂富(Bountiful)和海狸(Beaver)社区的公用事业公司退出一天后。尽管如此，就连批评人士也怀疑UAMPS的交易是否会立即破裂。今年8月，NuScale的设计通过了NRC审查过程中的一个关键里程碑，收到了安全评估报告，观察家们预计最终的“设计认证”将于明年到来。韦伯说，与此同时，UAMPS正在完成建造和运营工厂的申请。他说，申请应该在2023年提交，工厂的建设应该在2025年开始。
然而，韦伯说，在开工建设之前，UAMPS仍需排队购买该电厂全部720兆瓦的发电量。到目前为止，参与该项目的UAMPS成员已同意只接受产量中相对较小的一部分。因此，UAMPS可能不得不说服很多其他人相信这是一笔划算的交易。
note_en:
Plans to build an innovative new nuclear power plant—and thus revitalize the struggling U.S. nuclear industry—have taken a hit as in recent weeks:  Eight of the 36 public utilities that had signed on to help build the plant have backed out of the deal. The withdrawals come just months after the Utah Associated Municipal Power Systems (UAMPS), which intends to buy the plant containing  12 small modular reactors from NuScale Power, announced that completion of the project would be delayed by 3 years to 2030. It also estimates the cost would climb from $4.2 billion to $6.1 billion.
 “The project is still very much going forward,” says LaVarr Webb, a spokesperson for UAMPS, which has nearly four dozen members in Utah, California, Idaho, Nevada, New Mexico, and Wyoming. Although some UAMPS members have dropped out, “promising discussions are ongoing with a number of utilities to join the project or enter into power-purchase agreements,” Webb says.
 However, critics of the project say the developments underscore that the plant, which is designed by NuScale Power and would be built at the Department of Energy’s (DOE’s) Idaho National Laboratory, will be untenably expensive. M. V. Ramana, a physicist who works on public policy at the University of British Columbia, Vancouver, says he’s not surprised that so many utilities have opted out of the project. The question, he says, is why so many are sticking with it. “They ought to be seeing the writing on the wall and getting out by the dozens,” he says.
  NuScale, which was spun out of Oregon State University in 2007 and has benefited from DOE support, has designed a small modular nuclear reactor the company says will be safer, cheaper, and more flexible that a conventional gigawatt power reactor. Each of NuScale’s little reactors would produce just 60 megawatts of power. A plant would contain 12 of the modular reactors, which would be built in a factory and shipped to the plant site. By relying on natural convection to cool the reactors and eliminating many pumps and pipes, NuScale engineers say they have designed a reactor that’s even safer than a conventional power reactor.
 In addition, operators can ramp the plant’s output up and down by turning on and off the individual reactors. That capability makes it attractive to UAMPS as it tries to cut its reliance on coal-powered electrical plants and reduce emissions of heat-trapping carbon dioxide gas, Webbs says. UAMPS has turned increasingly to solar power and wind energy, he says, but because the Sun doesn’t always shine and the wind doesn’t always blow, utilities still need a supply of carbon-free energy on demand. “The idea is that nuclear will complement, augment, and back up your renewables,” Webb says.
 However, if the NuScale plant doesn’t run constantly at full output, it will be less efficient and even more expensive to operate, in terms of cost per megawatt hour (MWh) of energy, Ramana argues. Peter Bradford, a former member of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) and former chair of the state utility commissions in Maine and New York, says renewables coupled with short-term storage in batteries would likely be a cheaper means to even out the supply.
 Webb counters that energy markets in California have shown that without some flexible ability to produce electricity when renewable supplies dip, utilities must still rely on carbon-intensive coal. The deal protects UAMPS customers by specifying a maximum cost for electricity from the plant of $55 per MWh, Webb says, which should make it competitive with the future price of electricity from gas. DOE will help ensure that rate, he says, as it recently  finalized a plan to bear $1.4 billion of the cost of the plant. “If it’s more than $55 [per MWh] we will not build the plant,” he says.
 Bradford questions how reliable that reassurance can be. He notes that in the 1980s, Washington Public Power Supply System agreed to build several nuclear reactors in Washington that ran far overbudget and were never completed, leading to the biggest default on municipal bonds in U.S. history. Public utilities are particularly vulnerable to such risks, Bradford says, as other than ratepayers they have few sources of revenue that could be used to cover cost overruns. “Not only are there no deep pockets, there are no pockets,” he says.
 On 28 October, Heber Light & Power in Utah withdrew from the project, just 1 day after utilities in the Utah communities of Bountiful and Beaver pulled out. Still, even critics doubt the UAMPS deal will fall apart immediately. In August, the NuScale design passed a key milestone in the NRC review process, receiving its safety evaluation report, and observers expect final “design certification” to come next year. In the meantime, UAMPS is moving to complete an application to construct and operate the plant, Webb says. That application should be submitted in 2023, construction of the plant should start in 2025, he says.
 Before construction can start, however, UAMPS still has to line up customers to buy the full 720-megawatt output of the plant, Webb says. So far, UAMPS members involved in the project have agreed to take only a relatively small fraction of that output. So UAMPS may have to convince plenty of other folks that it’s a good deal.

即使在大流行期间，T-Mobile仍在继续增长。周四，这家美国第二大无线运营商宣布，其用户数量已突破1亿大关，新的用户总数达到1.04亿。
该公司第三季度每股收益和营收也超过分析师预期，每股收益为1美元，营收为193亿美元。根据雅虎财经(Yahoo Finance)的调查，分析师此前预计该季度每股收益为43美分，营收为183.4亿美元。
为我们的编辑订阅CNET Now时事通讯，精选当天最重要的新闻。
T-Mobile表示，该季度新增用户略高于200万，其中近198万是后付费净增加。后付费订户在月底付费，投资界将其视为衡量成功的关键指标，对他们的评价更高。该公司表示，它增加了68.9万名后付费电话用户。
除了财务更新，该公司表示，其覆盖全国的低频段5G网络现已覆盖2.7亿人，该公司更快的中频网络现已覆盖3000万人。T-Mobile技术总裁内维尔·雷(Neville Ray)此前告诉CNET，他预计到2020年底，中频网络将覆盖1亿人，到2021年底覆盖2亿人。
在周四公布财报之前，T-Mobile在今年早些时候与Sprint合并后，在上个季度超过AT&amp；T，成为美国第二大无线运营商。随着合并在4月份正式完成，T-Mobile表示，自那以来，它已经将15%的流量从Sprint的后付费用户转移到了自己的网络上。
这一财报发布之际，T-Mobile正处于忙碌时期。上周日，该公司对其TVision电视服务进行了调整，将重点放在了流媒体上，不过此后，该服务在哪些频道被纳入哪个服务等级的问题上遭到了Discovery、NBC和ViacomCBS等合作伙伴的批评。周三，T-Mobile子公司Sprint被美国联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)罚款2亿美元，作为Sprint涉嫌滥用联邦低收入客户生命线计划的和解协议的一部分。
T-Mobile首席财务官彼得·奥斯瓦尔迪克(Peter Osvaldik)表示，作为Sprint合并协议的一部分，罚款由Sprint的前所有者软银(Softbank)全额赔偿。
与此同时，T-Mobile正计划发布苹果(Apple)iPhone 12 Pro Max和12 Mini，这两款产品将于周五开始预购，11月13日开始销售。本周，T-Mobile推出了针对这两款设备的新优惠，加大了交易力度，包括增加一项转机优惠，其中包括两条不限量的Essentials服务，以及两条iPhone 12 Pro Max或两条iPhone 12 Pro Max，月费100美元。
T-Mobile预计第四季度将新增60万至70万后付费电话用户。

T-Mobile is continuing to grow, even amid the pandemic. On Thursday the nation's second-largest wireless carrier announced that it had crossed the 100-million mark for customers, with a new total of 100.4 million.
 The company also beat analyst estimates on earnings per share and revenue for the third quarter, posting EPS of $1 on revenues of $19.3 billion. Analysts, as polled by  Yahoo Finance, were expecting an EPS of 43 cents for the quarter and revenues of $18.34 billion.
  Subscribe to the CNET Now newsletter for our editors' picks of the most important stories of the day.
 T-Mobile said it had added just over 2 million new customers for the quarter, of which nearly 1.98 million were postpaid net additions. Postpaid subscribers, who pay at the end of the month, are valued more highly by the investment community as a key metric for success. The company says it added 689,000 postpaid phone users.
 In addition to the financial update, the company says its low-band nationwide  5G network now covers 270 million people with the company's faster midband network now being available to 30 million people. Neville Ray, T-Mobile's president of technology, previously told CNET that he   expects the midband network to reach 100 million people by the end of 2020 and to cover 200 million people by the end of 2021.
 Thursday's results follow T-Mobile passing AT&T last quarter to become the US' second-largest wireless provider, thanks to T-Mobile's merger with Sprint earlier this year. With the merger officially closed in April, T-Mobile says it has since moved 15 percent of traffic from Sprint's postpaid users onto its network.
 The earnings come amid a busy time for T-Mobile. On Sunday the company   revamped its TVision television service to focus on streaming, though it has since been met with  criticism from partners such as Discovery, NBC and ViacomCBS over which channels are included in which tier of service. T-Mobile subsidiary Sprint was   fined $200 million by the Federal Communications Commission on Wednesday as part of a settlement for Sprint's alleged abuses of a federal Lifeline program for low-income customers.
 T-Mobile Chief Financial Officer Peter Osvaldik says the fine is "fully indemnified" by Softbank, Sprint's prior owner, as part of the Sprint merger agreement.
 The earnings also come as T-Mobile is planning for the   release of Apple's iPhone 12 Pro Max and 12 Mini which are available for preorder on Friday before going on sale on Nov. 13. The carrier introduced new offers for the devices this week,   upping its deals, including adding a switcher offer that includes two lines of its unlimited Essentials service and either two iPhone 12 Pros or two iPhone 12 Pro Maxes for $100 a month.
 T-Mobile is expecting to add between 600,000 and 700,000 new postpaid phone customers in the fourth quarter.

title: T-Mobile报告第三季度营收为193亿美元，同比增长45.1%，超过分析师预测的183.4亿美元，总用户数超过1亿。
title_en: T-Mobile reports Q3 revenue of $19.3B, up 45.1% YoY, beating analysts estimates of $18.34B, as it passes 100M total customers
